title: Document TUI Operator Shortcuts
description: Update the README TUI section to describe the new operator keybindings, confirmation rules, scoped run behavior, continuous mode semantics, and status-panel action feedback.
acceptance_criteria: README documents `s`, `S`, `t`, `m`, and `u` shortcuts and their operator intent.
README explains which actions require confirmation and that disallowed actions do not mutate state.
README explains that run controls honor `--feature-root` when present and operate globally otherwise.
README describes the bottom status panel feedback for last action, success/failure result, and timestamp.

handoff_summary.completed: Revised README.md so the TUI docs now explain the operator intent for `s`, `S`, `t`, `m`, and `u`; clarify which actions require confirmation; state that invalid or disallowed actions do not mutate bead state; document that scheduler controls honor `--feature-root` when present and run globally otherwise; and describe the bottom status panel fields for last action, result, and timestamp.
handoff_summary.remaining: No documentation work remains in this bead.
handoff_summary.risks: README.md is the only touched file. Downstream agents should avoid rewriting the same TUI subsection without checking these wording updates against the current implementation.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Potential overlap if another bead edits the README Interactive TUI section concurrently.

conflict_risks: Potential overlap if another bead edits the README Interactive TUI section concurrently.

execution_history.summary: Updated the README TUI section to document operator shortcuts, confirmation requirements, scoped scheduler-run behavior, continuous mode semantics, and bottom status-panel action feedback.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise documentation changes and remaining gaps.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
